title: improve docs/manpages
body:
I am currently working on improving CRI-O's docs and think that libpod and podman can need some polishing as well. Take `podman-container(1)` as an example: the formatting is quite broken and it states `podman-container (1"")` instead of `podman-container(1)`.

As scary as it might sounds, I propose to go through each and every manpage and check for (cross) consistency, referential integrity and especially for a (consistent) format and layout.

Due to the broken format in `podman-container(1)` I consider this to be a bug.

comments.body:
I would like to help a little bit with this. I'm kind of new to containers and podman, so I'm reading the docs alot and I can give feedback on where they're not clear. I have detected a few errors that I can fix, as well as some things I can't immediately fix myself. 

I propose that when I come across an issue, either I create a pull request, or failing that I open a specific issue here and reference this one in either case.

Maybe a documentation label on the issues would be handy as well.

@najamelan Thanks for looking into this.

I have just opened a PR make the manpages more consistent: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1375

@baude 

I see that there are different ways of mentioning options with parameters:
1. `--mac-address=""` <- quotes, I imagine this means it takes a string?
2. `--memory-swap="LIMIT"` <- a named value, note that LIMIT is not what the user should write here
3. `--network="bridge"` <- bridge is an actual value here, why it is quoted is beyond me
4. `--oom-kill-disable=true|false` <- multiple choice
5. `--publish=[]` <- an array? does it need the brackets, I suppose not, just comma separated list?
6. `--stop-timeout=10` <- An actual value, the default one here. 
7. `--subgidname=name` a generic placeholder value. A bit like 2. but less confusing if you ask me.

I wonder if there is a preference. I could try to got through the man pages and make it consistent, but not before approval, because it will be a bit of work.
I personally prefer 6. An actual example that looks the same as when you would use the option, choosing the default if there is one. When there are different formats, I would put several lines, as in:
```
--pid=container:mycontainer
--pid=container:ae2f0e8e1f6a
--pid=mynamespace
--pid=host

Set the PID mode for the container

       Default is to create a private PID namespace for the container

              · container:<name|id>: join another container's PID namespace

              · host: use the host's PID namespace for the container. Note: the host mode gives the container full access to local PID and is therefore considered
                insecure.

              · ns: join the specified PID namespace
```
This seems much clearer to me than just:
`--pid=""`

I would also keep 4.
I consider the initial issue to be fixed. Please open dedicated issues if you wish more things to be fixed.
@vrothberg thanks for running with this, your changes were really nice improvements here.
